sections.paragraph:
Ἰσαάκ, ὁ (יִצְחָק; v.l. Ἰσάκ [B-D-F §39, 3]) indecl. (LXX, TestAbr, Test12Patr, ParJer; ApcrEzk P 1 verso 11; SibOr 2, 247; Philo; Just., Mel.—Joseph. and EpArist 48 Ἴσακος, ου [Ant. 1, 227]; EpArist 49 Ἴσαχος) Isaac, son of Abraham (Gen 21:3ff) Ac 7:8a; GJs 1:3. Father of Jacob, Ac 7:8b. Named w. him and Abraham (s. Ἀβραάμ and Ἰακώβ) Mt 8:11; Lk 13:28; B 8:4; IPhld 9:1; GJs 20:2 (not pap). God as the God of Abr., Is., and Jac. (Ex 3:6) Mt 22:32; Mk 12:26; Lk 20:37; Ac 3:13; 7:32; B 6:8. Bearer of the promises Ro 9:7 (Gen 21:12), 10; Gal 4:28; cp. Hb 11:9, 18 (Gen 21:12), 20. Husband of Rebecca Ro 9:10; B 13:2 (Gen 25:21), 3. Sacrifice of Isaac (Gen 22:1ff) Hb 11:17; Js 2:21; 1 Cl 31:3; B 7:3. In the genealogy of Jesus Mt 1:2; Lk 3:34.—HSchoeps, The Sacrifice of Isaac in Paul’s Theology: JBL 65, ’46, 385–92.—M-M. TW.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 480
